observations: FIX 1 CONFIRMED (5 previously-vacuous entries now genuinely fail pre-implementation): ran verification entries 10, 14, 15, 16, and 19 verbatim, byte-for-byte from .agent/contracts/029.json, against the current unimplemented tree (tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh confirmed absent via `ls`). All 5 now exit non-zero on an explicit 'MISSING tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh' / 'MISSING or empty tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh' guard, BEFORE reaching the `|| true`-swallowed invocation, undefined-function fallthrough, or bare negated-grep that caused rev0's false-passes. Then ran ALL 20 verification commands verbatim in one full pass: exactly 4 of 20 print PASS / exit 0 -- entry 4 (AT4, git diff --quiet on bootstrap.sh, real check, bootstrap.sh genuinely untouched), entry 11 (AT11, a real `bash tools/validate.sh` invocation, genuinely reporting the current, feature-independent 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'), entry 12 (the vlib_scope_guard scope check, confirmed via reading tools/verify_lib.sh lines 103-142 that it is a real allow-list diff against `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all`, trivially-but-genuinely clean because nothing has changed yet -- not a hardcoded stub), and entry 20 (git diff --quiet on docs/planning/DEBT.md, real check, genuinely untouched). All 4 are exactly the baseline-invariant set named in the review brief; none is a false-pass -- each is a real command whose current-tree result happens to already satisfy the criterion, and each would correctly flip to failing if the corresponding non-goal were violated (verified for entry 12 by reading vlib_scope_guard's allow-list-diff logic; for 4/11/20 by their real, executed git-diff/validate.sh output). The remaining 16 of 20 entries all correctly FAIL, matching the current true state of an unimplemented feature. No remaining vacuous false-pass.

FIX 2 CONFIRMED (fetch-depth:0 dropped, no refuted claim remains): grep of the full contract for 'fetch-depth' returns exactly two hits, both narrative -- design_notes.scope's files_modify entry, which now frames the OLD 'fatal: attempt to fetch/clone from a shallow repository' claim explicitly as rev0's REFUTED, historical justification and states the DECISION as 'fetch-depth: 0 is DROPPED from the new e2e_bootstrap job', and verification entry 18's acceptance_test text, which states 'no fetch-depth assertion is made, since fetch-depth:0 is deliberately NOT required on this job' -- and entry 18's actual command only asserts action-SHA parity (checkout@11bd719... and setup-python@0b93645e...), never fetch-depth. No residual assertion anywhere claims fetch-depth:0 is load-bearing for the NEW job. Independently re-reproduced the underlying git behavior as a light spot-check (per this review's own 'need not re-run full end-to-end' allowance): cloned this repo shallowly (--depth 1) into an isolated scratch dir, confirmed `git rev-parse --is-shallow-repository` = true, created a fresh annotated tag on that shallow checkout's own HEAD, then ran `git clone --depth 1 --branch <tag> file://<shallow checkout>` against it: exit 0, no fatal error (only a harmless 'is not a commit!' advisory), reconfirming the rev0 refutation holds. `git diff HEAD -- .github/workflows/compliance.yml` returns zero lines (byte-identical to the tracked validate.sh's job, i.e. the existing validate job's own unrelated fetch-depth:0 for its own C8 reason is untouched by this contract) -- confirmed the validate job is genuinely untouched, not merely claimed untouched.

FIX 3 CONFIRMED (mktemp portability, no root-path temp files): grepped the full contract JSON for every /tmp-shaped literal; every match traces back to a `mktemp` or `mktemp -d` invocation (entries 9/14/15/16/17's scratch/out variables), and design_notes.hermetic_mechanism explicitly documents the harness's own scratch dir as `mktemp -d`-produced. No hardcoded root-level path (e.g. no bare `/tmp_out`-style literal) appears anywhere in the 'verification' array.

FIX 4 CONFIRMED (NDEBT-004 ledger contradiction closed): read docs/planning/DEBT.md line 17 verbatim and compared it word-for-word against design_notes.ndebt_004_decision's quoted block. The two match (modulo purely typographic markdown differences -- section-sign vs 'Sec', an arrow glyph vs '-to-', and backtick code-spans present in the Markdown source but rendered as plain text in the JSON string -- with identical semantic content): DEBT.md's remediation column now reads 'DEFERRED beyond F-029 ... spec-scoped (product_spec_004 Sec 4.2) to bootstrap.sh's OWN --verify-only inject-to-verify assertions ... NOT validate.sh --payload ... deferred to a future self-compliance feature', which is exactly what the contract quotes and reconciles against. The contract's scope.files_create/files_modify list only tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh and .github/workflows/compliance.yml -- DEBT.md is not touched by this feature's own scope, and verification entry 20's real `git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/DEBT.md` (re-run directly, exit 0/PASS) confirms this. No remaining contradiction between the contract's design_notes and the ledger's own text.

FIX 5 CONFIRMED (evidence_convention off-by-one): re-read evidence_convention and directly counted the 'verification' array via `json.load` -- length is 20, and the convention text's illustrative example now reads '...029-verify-20.txt for the twentieth' with an explicit '20 entries' cross-reference, matching the actual count. No off-by-one remains.

NO REGRESSION / NO NEW DEFECT: none of the rev0-approved verification entries (1-9, 11-13, 17-18, 20) were altered in a way that changes their soundness -- entries 1-9,11-13,17,18,20 are textually identical or only cosmetically annotated (rev1 provenance notes) relative to rev0's already-approved logic, confirmed by re-running all 20 verbatim (see fix-1 observation above) and finding the same pass/fail pattern rev0 found for the unaffected entries, modulo the 5 intentionally-hardened ones. The Mode-A validator's approval (.agent/validator/029-mode-a.json, approved:true) of the NDEBT-004 'NO' decision as spec-faithful is unaffected by rev1's purely-reconciliation-focused edit to design_notes.ndebt_004_decision; the underlying (a)-(d) scope decision Mode A approved is unchanged.

CORE MECHANISM LIGHT SPOT-CHECK (unchanged from rev0, per this review's allowance not to re-run the full end-to-end): re-confirmed the file:// + ephemeral-annotated-tag clone path succeeds (see fix-2 observation's shallow-clone reproduction, which exercises exactly this path) and that the repository tree is left clean afterward (`git tag -l 'e2e-*'` empty, `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` showing only the four pre-existing .agent/ entries, both before and after this review's dry-runs). Nothing in rev1's diff touches design_notes.hermetic_mechanism, assertion_design, or negative_self_test_semantics (confirmed identical text to rev0 by direct comparison), so the previously-confirmed end-to-end mechanism is unchanged.

Housekeeping: all scratch git clones and tags created during this review's verification (under the session scratchpad directory, never inside the nizam-framework working tree) were removed before concluding; the nizam-framework repository tree was confirmed clean immediately before and after this review, containing only the four pre-existing untracked/modified .agent/ entries (.agent/run_state.json modified, .agent/contracts/029.json, .agent/qa/029-contract-review.json, .agent/validator/029-mode-a.json untracked) that predate this review session.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED (rev1). All rev0 blockers are verifiably fixed. (1) The 5 previously-vacuous verification entries (10, 14, 15, 16, 19) were re-run verbatim against the current, unimplemented tree and now all correctly exit non-zero, guarded by explicit existence/source/declare-F checks before any assertion is invoked; a full 20-entry dry-run confirms exactly 4 entries pass (AT4, AT11, the scope guard, and the DEBT.md-untouched check) and all 4 are genuinely baseline-invariant real checks, not false-passes -- no remaining vacuous entry. (2) fetch-depth:0 has been dropped from the new e2e_bootstrap job; no refuted shallow-clone justification remains as a live claim (only as explicitly-labeled refuted rev0 history); SHA-pin parity with the validate job is retained (verification entry 18) and the validate job is confirmed byte-unchanged (`git diff HEAD` returns zero lines); the shallow-clone-then-file://-clone-succeeds finding was independently re-reproduced as a light spot-check. (3) All temp-file usage across the verification suite is mktemp/mktemp -d based; no hardcoded root-level path remains. (4) design_notes.ndebt_004_decision now quotes docs/planning/DEBT.md's NDEBT-004 remediation text verbatim (confirmed by direct comparison against the live file), and the contradiction flagged at rev0 is closed; the feature's scope does not touch DEBT.md, confirmed by both the scope block and a live re-run of the git-diff check. (5) The evidence_convention off-by-one is corrected to 20 entries, matching the array's actual, directly-counted length. No new defect was introduced by the rev0-to-rev1 edits; all previously-sound entries remain sound. The core hermetic mechanism, already confirmed working end-to-end at rev0, is unchanged in this revision and was re-confirmed via a light spot-check of the ephemeral-tag + file:// clone path. All three blocking arrays (issues, missing_acceptance_coverage, unsupported_claims) are empty. The working tree was left exactly as found: no e2e-* git tags, git status showing only the four pre-existing .agent/ entries that predate this review.
